title: rubus rivularis subsp. aculeolatus
intro: Rubus rivularis subsp. aculeolatus, also known as the thorny raspberry or aculeolatus raspberry, is a subspecies of bramble in the rose family (Rosaceae). This plant has been found to have medicinal properties due to its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. The health benefits of Rubus rivularis subsp. aculeolatus include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and aid in the treatment of various diseases such as diabetes and certain types of cancer. The plant's medicinal properties can be prepared into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its benefits and promote overall well-being.
